shortDescription.text: Identifies content that is purely informational or instructional with no detected malicious intent.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags content that appears to be documentation, guides, or educational material. It confirms the absence of executable code, suspicious external calls, data exfiltration attempts, or system modification behaviors. While the file type (e.g., skill.md) can potentially host malicious payloads, this specific instance has been verified as benign based on static analysis and threat intelligence reconciliation.

message.text: Threat Intel CONFIRMED the legitimacy of external references (refactoring.guru). No suspicious lines were identified in the initial analysis. No confirmed malicious intent (exfiltration, exploitation, C2, persistence) was found. The content is purely informational and instructional, describing software design patterns. This behavior could belong to a legitimate tool.
